source.title: The highs and lows of beta activity in cortico-basal ganglia loops.

confidence_rationale: Directly addresses the BetaGate mechanism that ARC-028 wires to. Beta synchrony in cortico-BG loops acts as a computational capacity gate: high beta = locked-in/committed state, suppressed beta = open to new action selection. Dopamine suppresses beta (hence Parkinson's beta excess from dopamine depletion). ARC-028's claim that a dopamine-triggering completion signal can release BetaGate is directly supported by this mechanism. Confidence moderate because the paper primarily discusses pathological states (Parkinson's); normal-range beta modulation by hippocampal completion signals is inferred, not measured.
failure_signatures: Most beta-oscillation evidence in this paper is from Parkinson's disease pathology (dopamine depletion). Normal beta modulation during goal-directed navigation is less characterized.

Beta suppression by dopamine may be a threshold effect (large dopamine change) rather than a graded signal proportional to completion quality, which could limit ARC-028's graded hysteresis mechanism (MECH-105, MECH-106).

mapping.source_claim_statement: Increased beta band synchrony in cortico-BG circuits acts as an 'immutability promoting' gate that reinforces incumbent processes and limits computational capacity for novel action selection. Dopamine depletion pathologically enhances beta; normal phasic dopamine release suppresses beta to allow motor initiation.
mapping.ree_translation: BetaGate in REE implements exactly this: elevated beta = committed trajectory locked in; beta suppression triggered by dopamine (which is in turn triggered by hippocampal completion signal via NAc -> VP -> VTA) = gate release for next trajectory selection cycle. ARC-028 wires the source of this dopamine to HippocampalModule's completion signal.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The paper addresses motor initiation gating (suppression of competing motor programs), not trajectory selection gating per se. Whether the same beta mechanism gates discrete planning cycles (hippocampal sequence termination -> next CEM proposal) as opposed to motor output suppression is an extrapolation. The two may be implemented by different BG loops (MECH-069: incommensurable error signals across three loops).
mapping.source_context: EJN review synthesizing human and animal electrophysiology, primarily Parkinson's disease literature; normal beta modulation inferred from pharmacological and DBS data.

confidence_components.notes: Good quality review. Mapping fidelity reasonable -- beta-as-gate is directly the BetaGate mechanism. Transfer risk slightly elevated because the paper's evidence base is pathological (PD) and the normal-function inference is indirect.
